subject: Computer Networking
title: The Three-Way Handshake
description: A quick, practical guide to The Three-Way Handshake.

hook.text: Before two computers share a single byte, they perform a three-step introduction.

buildup.text: Step one: the client sends SYN — 'I want to connect.' Step two: the server replies SYN-ACK — 'Got it, ready.'

discovery.text: Step three: the client confirms with ACK. Now both sides know the connection is alive and mutual.

twist.text: Without this handshake, a server could waste resources on connections that never complete.

climax.text: SYN floods exploit this — sending fake SYNs to overwhelm a server. That's a denial-of-service attack.

punchline.text: SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK. Three steps to trust.

quiz.question: What is the correct order of a TCP three-way handshake?
